resources.string: Stock level graph showing medication inventory over time
Stock level graph showing %1$d medications over time
The medication name should be accurate to what you see on your label for your own reference. You can set a display name that will take precedence over the label name throughout the app.
Editing your medication name, strength or type also affects your history.\n\nIf you changed medication or dose add a new medication entry and archive this one.
Labels determine how you will get notified about your schedule.
You can edit these labels or add new ones later in settings.
Control how you're notified for each medication
Each medication has an importance label associated with it, that determines how you are notified about your schedule.
Turn a follow-up reminder into a critical alert if you still haven't logged your medication.
Standard notification that follows your device's notification settings.
Breaks through Do Not Disturb to ensure you never miss important medication.
Fullscreen alarm with looping sound and vibration. The strongest reminder type.
Alarm-level reminders use enhanced notifications on this device. For full alarm behavior (screen wake, Focus bypass, continuous sound), update to iOS 26 or later.
Escalate a follow-up reminder to a fullscreen alarm if you still haven't logged your medication.
Alarm notifications need permission to show over your lock screen. Please grant the fullscreen intent permission in settings.
Some fields have invalid values. Do you want to discard these changes or continue editing?
Are you sure you want to delete this importance label? This action cannot be undone.

plurals.name: importance_label_dialog_delete_blocked_message
plurals.item: This label is used by %1$d active medication. Please change its importance label first:
This label is used by %1$d active medications. Please change their importance label first:

resources.string: Archived medications using this label will be reassigned to the default label. This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s? This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to archive %1$s? You can restore it later from archived medications.
Are you sure you want to unarchive %1$s? It will be restored to your active medications.
Schedules determine when you should take this medication.
Some fields have invalid values. Do you want to discard these changes or continue editing?
Are you sure you want to delete this schedule? This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to archive this schedule? You can restore it later.
Are you sure you want to restore this schedule?
Make sure text is clear and readable
Make sure the label is clearly visible
No medication data could be detected
Camera permission is required to detect medication
You can edit this information in the following steps.
Please make sure these values are correct and as written on your medication. If not correct the data or skip.

Point your camera at a medication package or label. We'll detect the text and use it to pre-fill the next steps so you can add the medication faster. You'll still be able to review and edit everything before saving.

Text and object recognition run on your device using Google's ML Kit. ML Kit may contact Google's servers to receive updates and sends usage and performance metrics to Google. This is covered by the %1$s. The image itself is not sent to Google by ML Kit.
Text and object recognition run on your device using Apple's Vision framework. Use of these features is covered by Apple's %1$s.
Choose how the scanned text should be interpreted.
A standard text extractor. No AI and nothing to download, but it is less reliable.
A standard text extractor. Runs entirely on your device without AI, but it is less reliable.
An on-device AI model figures out the name, form, and dosage. Runs offline (the model may need to download the first time you use it).

Apple's AI figures out the name, form, and dosage. Apple runs this on your device when possible. For more complex requests, Apple may send the relevant data to Apple's Private Cloud Compute, where Apple states the data is not stored or accessible to them. %1$s.
The scanned text will be processed on your device using a standard text extractor.
Tip: Try updating Google Play Services and restarting your device.
Tip: Gemini Nano may still be initializing. This can take a few minutes after device setup. Try restarting your device or waiting a bit longer.
Tip: Check your internet connection and try again in a few minutes.
Save your medications and settings to a backup file
Restore medications from a backup file
Back up all medications daily with encryption
Your backups are encrypted with this passphrase. You will need it to restore from a backup.
Write this passphrase down! Without it, your backups cannot be restored.
Storage location no longer accessible. Please select a new folder.
Backups are stored in iCloud Drive and sync automatically across your devices.
Set a passphrase to enable automatic backups

HelloMeds automatically backs up all your medications daily with encryption. If you ever lose access to your device, you can restore from a backup using your passphrase.\n\nBackups are encrypted so only you can read them. Choose a strong passphrase and write it down somewhere safe.
Set up automatic backups to keep your medication data safe. If you ever lose access to this device, you can restore everything with your passphrase.
Also export medications you have archived
Export taken/skipped records and stock adjustments. May increase file size.
Select a HelloMeds backup file (.json) to import. You'll be able to review what will be imported before confirming.
Found %1$d medication(s), %2$d schedule(s), and %3$d importance label(s)
A medication with this name already exists
Protect your backup with a passphrase using AES-256 encryption

Encryption protects your backup only as well as your passphrase. Short or common passphrases can be guessed by someone with access to the file. Use a long, unique passphrase for meaningful protection.
This backup file is encrypted. Enter the passphrase to decrypt it.
Use your camera to automatically fill in medication details from labels
Basic scan: Quick pattern-based scanning\nGemini: AI-powered detection (Gemini Nano)
Important medication reminders that require immediate attention
Fullscreen alarm reminders that wake the screen and play a looping sound
Low stock warnings and measurement reminders
Reminders when a medication container is likely empty
Receive reminders to take your medications
Enable notifications in system settings to receive medication reminders.
Configure sound and vibration for each notification type
Configure sound, vibration, and display for regular reminders
Configure alarm sound and verify Do Not Disturb bypass for critical medications
Medication names are visible in notifications
Notifications are hidden when the device is locked
Ensures notifications arrive at exact scheduled time
When disabled, reminders will be delivered with a delay of around 15 minutes. Potentially longer depending on battery saver or optimization settings.
Most users don't need to change this. Only disable battery optimization if notifications are consistently late or missing, even after granting exact alarm permission.
HelloMeds is a personal medication tracking tool. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
Always follow your healthcare provider's instructions regarding your medication schedule and dosage.
Your data never leaves your device. HelloMeds works entirely offline and all medication data is stored locally. Only manual backups you create can be shared.
Allow HelloMeds to send you medication reminders at the right time.
Allow exact alarm scheduling to ensure your reminders arrive precisely when you need them, not just approximately.
Use your camera or add medication you want to track manually.
Each medication has a label that determines how you will get notified about your schedule.
Add the times you need to take your medication, and HelloMeds will remind you based on your label.
Notifications are required for medication reminders. If you disable them, you won't receive any reminders from the app.

Using precise notifications lets the app run in the background, which may use more battery.\n\nImprecise notifications can be delayed, usually by up to 15 minutes. Potentially longer depending on your phone's battery optimizations.
You're ready to set up your first medication
Scan medication packaging to automatically detect details
Restore medications from a backup file
Notification permission has been revoked. HelloMeds cannot send you medication reminders without this permission.
Exact alarm permission has been revoked. Your medication reminders may not arrive at the precise time scheduled.
Critical notifications can't bypass Do Not Disturb
Some of your medications use critical reminders that need to break through Do Not Disturb mode. Enable this in notification channel settings.
Adapt app colors to your wallpaper (Android 12+)
Prevent screenshots and hide content in the app switcher
Get in touch and generate a diagnostic report
Running into trouble or have feedback? You can reach out by email at %1$s. If you are more technically inclined, you can also file an issue on GitHub.

You do not need to send a report right away. It can be helpful later when tracking down a specific bug. The report includes system information, notification settings, and scheduling state — medication names and personal notes are not included. Even so, it is not completely free of personal details, so please review it before sharing and remove anything you are not comfortable with.
Launch onboarding with smart permission skipping (granted permissions are hidden)
Show all onboarding screens regardless of permission status
Enable stock tracking in medication settings to monitor inventory
Track your medication inventory to get low stock alerts and usage insights.
How would you like to proceed?
How do you want to track stock?
Use this when you know exactly how much medication you take each time, such as pills, patches or accurately measured liquids. Each dose reduces your supply by a known amount.
Use this for medications in dispensers, tubes, or inhalers. Track how many containers you have — the app estimates when each one runs out based on your schedule.
What type of container does your medication come in?
If you know how many doses are in each container, we can estimate when it runs out and remind you to update your stock.
We can remind you to update your stock when we think a container is used up based on your dose schedule.
If your medication comes in fixed-amount containers, you can add this here to make tracking easier
How much do you have right now?
Do you want to be notified when you're low on stock?
Estimated to run out between %1$s and %2$s
This will reduce your container count by one. You can undo this by updating your stock.
Helps estimate when your current container will run out
Reminds you to update your stock when a container is likely used up
Fine-tune the current fill level of your open container
Set up a schedule to adjust remaining doses
Add a schedule to see predictions
Your supply is running low. Tap to view stock details.
You've taken %1$d doses since the last container change. Tap to update your stock.
Enter the total amount in your container and the amount per dose. We'll calculate how many doses it contains.
Dose amount can't exceed total container amount
Enable notifications in system settings to receive medication reminders.
Allow exact alarms so your reminders arrive on time, not minutes late.
Allow full-screen alerts so your screen turns on for medication reminders.
Critical medication reminders won't break through Do Not Disturb. Enable DnD bypass in channel settings.
Some of your medications use alarm-level reminders. Enable alarm permission so they can wake your screen and bypass Focus mode.
Some of your medications use critical reminders. Enable critical alerts so they can play a sound even on silent or Do Not Disturb.
Automatic backups have failed %d times in a row. Check your backup settings.
Allow HelloMeds to turn on your screen when a medication reminder fires — even when your phone is locked.
Allow HelloMeds to use system alarms for your most important medications. Alarms can wake your screen and play a sound even when your phone is in Focus mode.
Without alarm permission, important medication reminders will still be delivered as notifications but may not wake your screen or bypass Focus mode.
Allow HelloMeds to send critical alerts for medications marked as critical. These alerts play a sound even when your phone is on silent or Do Not Disturb.
Critical alerts are only used for medications you mark as critical. Without this permission, critical medications will use standard time-sensitive notifications instead.
Ideally use the name on the packaging or bottle. You can add a display name later.
What form does your medication come in.
Optional strength of your medication. Should be on your packaging or bottle.
Helps you recognize your medication in the app.
Choose a color to customize your medication icon.
Passphrase must be at least 8 characters
This backup was created by a newer version of HelloMeds. Some data may not import correctly.
Unknown medication type '%1$s', will use Tablet
Unknown strength unit '%1$s', will be cleared
Schedule %1$d: unknown frequency type '%2$s', will use Interval
Unknown tracking precision '%1$s', will be cleared
Unknown container type '%1$s', will be cleared
Importance label '%1$s' not found, will use default
Configure timing behavior and dosing cycles for this medication.
Adjusts your schedule and notifications based on active and break days.
During break days, you will still receive reminders for placebo pills. Notifications will be quieter.
During break days, no reminders will be sent and no events will appear on your schedule.
Pack includes placebo pills during break
If you started today, you are on day 1.
This will reset your cycle to Day 1.
The cycle advances by one each calendar day from your start date — it does not pause for missed doses or non-scheduled days.
Your schedule sets the specific times and days for your medication. Your cycle acts as an overall filter for those reminders.
On Active days, you'll receive your reminders as planned. On Break days, those same reminders are either skipped or switched to placebos.
You have a missed active dose. Check your medication instructions for guidance.
Keeps exact timing when you travel
Recommended for birth control or antibiotics where exact intervals matter
Reminders are anchored to this timezone
Too many reminders scheduled. Open the app every 2 days to keep them active.
A system error caused your local database to be wiped to prevent a fatal crash. Please restore your medications from a backup or re-enter them.
